definition: A literature source supports a design-relevant causal or structural claim.
A reactor architecture contains a named component.
A component, feed, or operating condition improves a performance or transport function.
A component, feed, or operating condition introduces or increases a failure risk.
A failure mode is caused by a transport bottleneck, operating condition, or component limitation.
A diagnosis, failure mode, or claim is evidenced by measured voltage, EIS, FE, photo, or similar evidence.
A next design or component replaces a previous bottleneck component.
